_source: Gen 2 Compendiums/ALGEBRA AND MATRICES - VOL 1 (algebra) & VOL 2 (matrices), TASC MTG215123 Module 2 Topic 1. Answer keys verified by tools/verify_answers.py.

unit.title: Algebra & Matrices
unit.subtitle: Module 2 · Topic 1
unit.description: Evaluate and rearrange linear and non-linear expressions, and use matrices to store information and solve problems: order and types of matrices, addition, subtraction, scalar multiplication, matrix multiplication and powers. Criteria C5 (algebra & matrices) and C1.

lessons.title: Substitution & evaluating formulas
lessons.overview: Finding the value of an expression when the variables are known.

sections.title: Substitution
sections.html:
Substitution replaces each variable (pronumeral) with its value, then evaluates. To evaluate 3x + 5 when x = 4: 3×4 + 5 = 17. Non-linear expressions work the same way — evaluate x² − 2x when x = 5: 25 − 10 = 15.

Follow the order of operations: brackets, indices, then multiply/divide, then add/subtract.

sections.title: Substituting into real formulas
sections.html:
Formulas relate quantities. Examples:

  • Temperature: C = 5/9(F − 32). At F = 68: 5/9×36 = 20°C.
  • Body Mass Index: BMI = weight ÷ height². For 72 kg and 1.8 m: 72 ÷ 3.24 = 22.22.

sections.title: Tables of values with a spreadsheet
sections.html: A spreadsheet formula (always starting with =) can evaluate the same formula for many inputs at once. Entering =5/9*(A2-32) and filling down builds a whole table of Celsius values from a column of Fahrenheit values — far faster than repeating the calculation by hand.

lessons.title: Transposition: changing the subject
lessons.overview: Rearranging a formula so a different variable is the subject.

sections.title: Making a variable the subject
sections.html:
Transposition rearranges an equation so an alternative variable becomes the subject. Do the same operation to both sides, undoing operations in reverse order.

To make x the subject of y = 3x + 6: subtract 6 (y − 6 = 3x), then divide by 3, giving x = (y − 6) ÷ 3. Now any y gives x directly — at y = 18, x = 12 ÷ 3 = 4.

sections.title: Worked example
sections.html: The perimeter of a rectangle is P = 2(l + w). To find the width: P ÷ 2 = l + w, so w = P÷2 − l. If P = 30 and l = 8, then w = 15 − 8 = 7.

lessons.title: Matrices: order, types & elements
lessons.overview: What a matrix is and how to describe one.

sections.title: What is a matrix?
sections.html:
A matrix is a rectangular array of numbers in rows and columns, used to store and organise information (databases, prices, networks).

The order (or dimensions) of a matrix is rows × columns — always rows first. A matrix with 2 rows and 3 columns has order 2 × 3.

sections.title: Elements and special matrices
sections.html:
Each number is an element, located by its (row, column) position — row first. Types you must recognise:

  • Row matrix: one row. Column matrix: one column.
  • Square matrix: equal rows and columns.
  • Zero matrix: every element is 0.
  • Identity matrix: 1s on the leading diagonal, 0s elsewhere — the matrix equivalent of the number 1.

sections.title: Addition and subtraction
sections.html:
You can add or subtract two matrices only if they have the same order. Add (or subtract) corresponding elements:

[1 2; 3 4] + [5 6; 7 8] = [6 8; 10 12].

sections.title: Scalar multiplication
sections.html:
To multiply a matrix by a scalar (a single number), multiply every element by that scalar:

3 × [1 2; 0 4] = [3 6; 0 12].

lessons.title: Matrix multiplication, powers & networks
lessons.overview: Multiplying matrices and using them to model.

sections.title: Matrix multiplication
sections.html:
To multiply matrices, take each row of the first with each column of the second: multiply matching entries and add. For 2×2 matrices:

[1 2; 3 4] × [5 6; 7 8] = [ (1×5+2×7) (1×6+2×8); (3×5+4×7) (3×6+4×8) ] = [19 22; 43 50].

The number of columns of the first must equal the number of rows of the second.

sections.title: Powers and applications
sections.html: A power of a square matrix means repeated multiplication: A² = A × A. Matrices model costing problems (quantities × prices) and networks — an adjacency matrix records direct links, and squaring it counts the two-step connections (e.g. how many ways two people can communicate via a third person).

quizzes.title: Quiz: Substitution & transposition
quizzes.overview: Evaluating and rearranging expressions. Unlimited attempts.

questions.prompt: Find the BMI of a person weighing 72 kg and 1.8 m tall, using BMI = weight ÷ height².
Make x the subject of y = 3x + 6, then find x when y = 18.
The perimeter of a rectangle is P = 2(l + w). Find w when P = 30 and l = 8.
Rearranging a formula so a different variable is the subject is called ______.
questions.explain: Rearranging to change the subject is transposition.

quizzes.title: Quiz: Matrices — order, types & basic operations
quizzes.overview: Describing matrices and adding, subtracting and scaling them. Unlimited attempts.

questions.prompt: A matrix has 2 rows and 3 columns. State its order (write it like 2x3).
In the matrix [4 6 1; 3 2 5], what is the element in row 2, column 1?
questions.explain: Row 2 is (3 2 5); its first entry is 3.

questions.prompt: Which of these is the 2×2 identity matrix?
questions.explain: The identity matrix has 1s on the leading diagonal and 0s elsewhere: [1 0; 0 1].

quizzes.title: Quiz: Matrix multiplication & powers
quizzes.overview: Multiplying matrices, powers and simple applications. Unlimited attempts.

questions.prompt: Find [1 1; 0 1]² (that is, the matrix multiplied by itself).
A cafe sells 3 coffees at $5 and 2 muffins at $4. Using the row matrix [3 2] and price column [5; 4], find the total takings as a 1×1 matrix.
You can multiply matrix A (order 2×3) by matrix B only if B has:
questions.explain: The columns of A (3) must equal the rows of B, so B must have 3 rows.

questions.prompt: Squaring a network's adjacency matrix counts the connections that use how many steps? (Type the number as a word or digit.)
questions.explain: A² counts two-step (via one intermediate) connections in a network.
